title: exostema longiflorum
intro: Exostema longiflorum, also known as the Jamaica dogwood or poison tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative effects, derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. Exostema longiflorum has been used to alleviate various health issues, such as anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms, as well as to reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for consumption, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
